text.title: Onion Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every arch-pattern-onion application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The Domain Model layer MUST have zero dependencies on any application, infrastructure, or presentation concern. Domain Services MAY depend on the Domain Model only. Application Services MAY depend on Domain and Domain Services. Infrastructure MAY depend on all inner layers.
rule.rationale: All coupling toward the center ensures that inner layers can be unit-tested without any external infrastructure. This is the defining invariant of Onion Architecture.

rule.statement: All coupling MUST point toward the centre. Inner layers (Domain Model, Domain Services) MUST NOT import from outer layers (Application, Infrastructure, UI).
rule.rationale: Onion's only non-negotiable; without it the pattern collapses to layered.

rule.statement: When the domain needs an external concern (persistence, messaging), it declares an interface in the Domain Services layer; Infrastructure implements that interface.
rule.rationale: This is what makes Onion testable; without it the domain is unreachable from a test without the full stack.

rule.statement: Onion has exactly four layers: Domain Model, Domain Services, Application Services, Infrastructure/UI/Tests. Adding fifth layers (Common.*, Shared.*) is forbidden — they become dump grounds.
rule.rationale: Common/Shared layers attract cross-cutting code and erode the dependency rule.

rule.statement: An import-direction lint MUST run in CI and block PRs that violate the inward-coupling rule.
rule.rationale: Without lint enforcement the pattern decays in 1-2 quarters.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
